threads.subject: TeraCorp refund request after Nov 3 outage

messages.body: Opening a formal thread after Slack. TeraCorp is a $48k/year account. They are asking for a two-month refund or equivalent credit because the Nov 3 outage disrupted a customer launch window.
I reviewed the incident notes. Customer-facing degradation lasted roughly four hours, though the raw timeline reads closer to 3h58m. TeraCorp had repeated escalations during that window.
Pushing back on the amount. Two months is outside policy and well above what we have granted on similar outages. I can support 30 days of service credit, not a cash refund.

Final call: treat this as a strategic retention exception. Offer six weeks of service credit, not cash, and position it as recognition of the outage plus a reset of trust. Marcus sends the note after Priya reviews wording.
Understood. I will tell TeraCorp we cannot do the full two months they demanded, but we can extend a six-week credit approved by leadership.

threads.subject: RE: Service interruption and next steps

messages.body: The Nov 3 outage lasted most of the morning for our team, cost us a planned rollout, and damaged confidence. We expect two months refunded.
I am escalating this internally now. I will come back with a reviewed response once finance and leadership weigh in.
